name: NO2 Air Quality Classifier
description: A Random Forest binary classifier that predicts whether hourly NO2 concentrations at Austrian monitoring stations are elevated (>= 40 µg/m³) or normal (< 40 µg/m³). The model is trained on temporal and station features derived from EEA air quality measurements.

ml:hyperparameters.target_definition: Value >= 40.0 µg/m³ is classified as elevated

ml:trainingData.name: EEA NO2 Air Quality Measurements Austria 2024
ml:trainingData.description: Hourly NO2 measurements from 16 Austrian monitoring stations sourced from the European Environment Agency data hub.

ml:features.name: hour
ml:features.description: Hour of day extracted from measurement start time (0-23)

ml:features.name: month
ml:features.description: Month of year extracted from measurement start time (1-12)

ml:features.name: weekday
ml:features.description: Day of week extracted from measurement start time (0=Monday, 6=Sunday)

ml:features.name: Validity
ml:features.description: EEA validity flag for the measurement (-1, 0, 1, 2, 3)

ml:features.name: Verification
ml:features.description: EEA verification flag for the measurement (0, 1, 2)

ml:features.name: data_capture_missing
ml:features.description: Binary flag indicating whether DataCapture value is missing

ml:targetVariable.name: no2_elevated
ml:targetVariable.description: Binary label: 1 (elevated) if NO2 value >= 40 µg/m³, 0 (normal) otherwise
ml:targetVariable.note: The measured Value column is used to create the label but is NOT used as a model feature to avoid data leakage

ml:classDistribution.note: Dataset is highly imbalanced — only 6.7% of measurements are elevated

ml:intendedUse: This model is intended for research and educational purposes as part of a FAIR Data Science course at TU Wien. It demonstrates how temporal and station-based features can be used to classify NO2 pollution levels at Austrian monitoring stations. It may also serve as a baseline model for air quality monitoring research.
ml:outOfScopeUse: This model should not be used for real-time air quality monitoring, public health decision-making, or regulatory compliance purposes. It is not validated for deployment in production environments.
ml:limitations: The dataset is highly imbalanced (only 6.7% elevated readings), which affects precision for the elevated class (0.21). The model has high recall (0.73) but low precision for elevated predictions.
Features are limited to temporal variables and station identifiers — no meteorological variables (wind, temperature, humidity) are included, which are known to influence NO2 levels.
The model is trained on data from 16 Austrian stations only and may not generalise to other regions or countries.
The 40 µg/m³ threshold is based on the WHO interim target — results would differ with a different threshold.
ml:ethicalConsiderations: The input data is publicly available from the EEA and does not contain personal information. No sensitive attributes are used as features. The model predictions should not be used to make decisions affecting individuals or communities without expert review.
